title: "Can't get wireless to work on HP dv6226"
date: 2012-05-17
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by u-stu.com on 2012-05-17
Based on other posts, here is the info you'll probably need to help me:

lspci -nnk | grep -iA2 net
```
02:00.0 Network controller [0280]: Broadcom Corporation BCM4311 802.11b/g WLAN [14e4:4311] (rev 01)
	Subsystem: Hewlett-Packard Company BCM4311 802.11b/g Wireless LAN Controller [103c:1363]
	Kernel modules: ssb
--
05:08.0 Ethernet controller [0200]: Intel Corporation PRO/100 VE Network Connection [8086:1092] (rev 02)
	Subsystem: Hewlett-Packard Company Device [103c:30bb]
	Kernel driver in use: e100
```

iwconfig```

lo        no wireless extensions.

eth0      no wireless extensions.
```

lsmod
```

### Post by kc1di on 2012-05-17
could you post the output of ```
lspci
``` l is lowercase L not #1.

---

### Post by chili555 on 2012-05-17
Which version are you running? ```
lsb_release -a
```If it's 12.04, hook up the ethernet, get a connection and do:```
sudo apt-get install linux-firmware-nonfree
sudo modprobe -r b43
sudo modprobe b43
```Unplug the ethernet and enjoy your wireless.

If it isn't 12.04, post the version and we'll help.

---

### Post by praseodym on 2012-05-17
Use the native driver instead of the STA. Deactivate it and install the missing firmware via cable:
```

sudo modprobe -rfv wl
sudo apt-get remove --purge bcmwl-kernel-source
wget http://media.cdn.ubuntu-de.org/forum/attachments/04/32/2480236-Broadcom_Firmware.tar.gz 
sudo tar xvf 2480236-Broadcom_Firmware.tar.gz -C /lib/firmware/ 
sudo modprobe -v b43
```
Edit: Check:
```
dmesg | grep b43
iwconfig
```
Maybe the firmware from "linux-firmware-nonfree" is too new. (dmesg will show it)

---

### Post by cdmc on 2013-01-20
Thanks to chili555 I got my Broadcomm modem working on 12.04.1, but whenever I boot my machine I have to use modprobe again to kickstart the modem. Is something missing in a configuration file? modprobe gives me an error message "WARNING: All config files need .conf: /etc/modprobe.d/ndiswrapper, it will be ignored in a future release." The modem then starts and auto connects to my wireless network. Any help in eliminating the need to use modprobe on each reboot would be appreciated.

---

### Post by chili555 on 2013-01-20
> **cdmc said:**
> Thanks to chili555 I got my Broadcomm modem working on 12.04.1, but whenever I boot my machine I have to use modprobe again to kickstart the modem. Is something missing in a configuration file? modprobe gives me an error message "WARNING: All config files need .conf: /etc/modprobe.d/ndiswrapper, it will be ignored in a future release." The modem then starts and auto connects to my wireless network. Any help in eliminating the need to use modprobe on each reboot would be appreciated.Do you have the exact same device as above?```
Broadcom Corporation BCM4311 802.11b/g WLAN [14e4:4311] (rev 01)
```Until I know that, I'm hesitant to suggest a fix. Also, we ought to find and fix this:> WARNING: All config files need .conf: /etc/modprobe.d/ndiswrapper, it will be ignored in a future release.Please run and post:```
cat /etc/modprobe.d/ndiswrapper
```What is it that you modprobe to get it all working?

---

### Post by cdmc on 2013-01-21
First, here is the output you requested:

****@laptop:~$ cat /etc/modprobe.d/ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004303sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004310sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004311sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004312sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004313sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004314sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004315sv0000E003sd0000105Bbc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004315sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004316sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004318sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004319sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d0000431Asv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004320sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004321sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004324sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004328sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d00004329sv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d0000432Asv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d0000432Bsv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper
alias pci:v000014E4d0000432Csv*sd*bc*sc*i* ndiswrapper

****@laptop:~$ 

**********************************************************************************

I tried the suggestion in the "wireless trouble shooting" section of the User Guide of putting the driver in "airplane mode," but that had no effect on the outcome. The system simply does not see a wireless modem (even though the manual switch is in the "On" position) until I do a "sudo modprobe b43".

********************************************************************************

I am not sure how to get the detail about the modem you asked for in Ubuntu, but attached is the information that Windows produced (It is a dual boot machine running Unbuntu and WIndows Vista.)

[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/jpeg;base64,[/IMG]

---

### Post by chili555 on 2013-01-21
Let's do some house-keeping. First, let's get rid of the needless ndiswrapper:```
sudo su
apt-get remove --purge ndiswrapper*
rm /etc/modprobe.d/ndiswapper [COLOR="Red"]<--it may already be gone as a result of the purge, please continue[/COLOR]
echo b43 >> /etc/modules
exit
```Reboot and tell us how beautifully it's now working!

---

### Post by cdmc on 2013-01-22
Removing ndiswrapper got rid of the error message; however, I still have to start the modem manually with a "sudo modprobe b43" after I login to get the network going.

After double checking, I discovered a typo in my change to /etc/modules. Having corrected that, now everything is working correctly!

Many thanks!!

---

### Post by chili555 on 2013-01-22
> **cdmc said:**
> Removing ndiswrapper got rid of the error message; however, I still have to start the modem manually with a "sudo modprobe b43" after I login to get the network going.

After double checking, I discovered a typo in my change to /etc/modules. Having corrected that, now everything is working correctly!

Many thanks!!Awesome! Glad it's working!

---

### Post by david_srulevitch on 2013-08-18
I'm having a similar issue, and tried chili555's instructions, but no luck. I'm not confident that I did it correctly, though. Complete novice, so I may have missed something.

here's what my device is listed as from doing some of the troubleshooting in the help menu :   Broadcom Corporation BCM4311 802.11b/g WLAN (rev 01)

I just installed 12.04 LTS and erased Vista.

The wireless switch at the front of the laptop shows an amber light whether the switch is in the left or right position. 

Thanks in advance for any help

also, here is some more data from troubleshooting instructions:

dsrulevitch@dsrulevitch-HP-Pavilion-dv6000-RP297UA-ABA:~$ iwconfig
lo        no wireless extensions.

eth0      no wireless extensions.

dsrulevitch@dsrulevitch-HP-Pavilion-dv6000-RP297UA-ABA:~$ sudo lshw -C network

       bus info: pci@0000:05:08.0

       resources: irq:20 memory:d8000000-d8000fff ioport:4000(size=64)
dsrulevitch@dsrulevitch-HP-Pavilion-dv6000-RP297UA-ABA:~$

---

### Post by chili555 on 2013-08-18
> I'm having a similar issue, and tried chili555's instructions, but no luck.Please do:```
sudo apt-get remove --purge bcmwl-kernel-source
```Now check for the firmware:```
ls /lib/firmware/b43
```Do you get dozens of entries or 'not found?' If not found, do:```
sudo apt-get install linux-firmware-nonfree
```Detach the ethernet, reboot and give us an update.

---

### Post by david_srulevitch on 2013-08-18
Thanks! that did the trick. In case it's helpful or matters, here's the log:

   dsrulevitch@dsrulevitch-HP-Pavilion-dv6000-RP297UA-ABA:~$ sudo apt-get remove --purge bcmwl-kernel-source  
 [sudo] password for dsrulevitch:  
 Reading package lists... Done  
 Building dependency tree        
 Reading state information... Done  
 The following package was automatically installed and is no longer required:  
   dkms  
 Use 'apt-get autoremove' to remove them.  
 The following packages will be REMOVED: 
   bcmwl-kernel-source*  
 0 upgraded, 0 newly installed, 1 to remove and 0 not upgraded.  
 After this operation, 3,656 kB disk space will be freed.  
 Do you want to continue [Y/n]? y  
 (Reading database ... 171132 files and directories currently installed.)  
 Removing bcmwl-kernel-source ...  
 Removing all DKMS Modules  
 ls /lib/firmware/b43  
 Done.  
 update-initramfs: deferring update (trigger activated)  
 Purging configuration files for bcmwl-kernel-source ...  
 update-initramfs: deferring update (trigger activated)  
 Processing triggers for initramfs-tools ...  

 update-initramfs: Generating /boot/initrd.img-3.5.0-37-generic  

Thanks again chili!
